notes:
Game works well but requires 2 Workarounds: 

1. Install DirectX with Wine (terminal: WINEPREFIX="/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx" wine "/home/$USER/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe" 

2. Add DirectX 9 to Anno's Engine.ini file (/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/Engine.ini). Add: <InitFile> %here is something else% <DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion> </InitFile>
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: Runs perfectly after applying the tweaks mentioned further down
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: Runs perfectly after applying the tweaks (Installing DX9 and setting it as the default) or using the Lutris installer. Multiplayer (local LAN) works flawlessly, the game performance is on par with Windows and I saw zero glitches with video or audio.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: Runs perfectly after applying the 2 workarounds for DX9 (mentioned before from others). Played serveral hours on high settings.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: I used the two workarounds mentioned below - #1 Installing wine and winetricks, then I executed this command in the terminal to manually install DirectX9 to Proton: WINEPREFIX= "<your-steam-directory>/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx" winetricks d3dx9 #2 You need to make the mentioned changes in Engine.ini in <your-steam-directory>/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/ - For that just add <DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion>  between the <InitFile> ... </InitFile> tags. BTW the game also works with bumblebee and Nvidia Optimus cards (mine is a GTX 960M; Driver: NVIDIA 415.27). After these changes the base game and the addon work perfectly fine except for the fonts which are slightly thinner than usual, but readable. All in all it runs smoothly.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes:
To get the game to run it required changing the DirectXVersion to 9 - and installing the provided DirectX redist into the prefix. Plenty of other comments have detailed the exact procedure.
With the DirectX changes applied though the game seems to run with native-like performance and without a lack of visible issues.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes:
Using the DX9 tweaks helps starting the game, but after loading a savegame from Windows it sometimes crashes immediately or crashes randomly within the game if you click something. 

Special environment info: I'm using the Windows savefiles and profile. And also the game is installed on ntfs, which I mount via ntfs-3g.

notes: Workaround for blackscreen: 1. install directX to wine (copy and paste code line into console: WINEPREFIX="/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx" wine "/home/$USER/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe" 2. add directX 9 to Anno's Engine.ini /home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/Engine.ini add: <InitFile> %here is something else% <DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion> </InitFile>

notes: Like described in the other posts, you have to configure directx9 inside the engine ini files. Disable D3D11. AND for me, the 4GB fix was very important to get the game stable. May that doesn't depends on wine, but good to know, I guess. Check here: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=577231025. I used the "Explorer Suite" (https://ntcore.com/), because of the "4GB Patch" executable doesn't work with wine for me.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: The game shows only a black screen out of the box. Had to install manually DX9, edit the Engine.ini and disable D3D11 to be able to play. But the game showed after some time graphical glitches and you need to restart the game. Would be nice if this game would work with DXVK or if Wine could make this at least stable.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes:
I had some issues finding the correct path for the following tweaks but this worked for me, step 1: 
 WINEPREFIX="/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx" wine "/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe" ... Wine throwed a couple of warnings but it worked after proceeding to step 2: add directX 9 to Anno's Engine.ini in /home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/Engine.ini add: <InitFile> %here is something else% <DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion> </InitFile>... it finally worked with maxed out settings, but i could only select anisotropic filtering max 4x. Starting with 8x the game would crash again. Happy gaming! P.S.: Lutris installer didn't work for me :(
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes:
Had to disable DirectX11:
1. install wine (if not already installed)
2. install DX9 WINEPREFIX="/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx" wine "/home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe"
3. insert into /home/$USER/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno 1404/Engine.ini:
<InitFile>
...
<DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion> 
...
</InitFile>

notes: The game shows only a black screen out of the box. Had to install manually DX9, edit the Engine.ini and disable D3D11 to be able to play. But the game showed after some time graphical glitches and you need to restart the game. Would be nice if this game would work with DXVK or if Wine could make this at least stable. Installed manually D9VK, it works as well but you need the laa patch until Wine fixes the 4gb limit.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: To get it running run the command: WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx/ wine ~/.steam/s/steamapps/common/Anno\ 1404/DirectX/DXSETUP.exe to install DirectX9 manually. Add the line <DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion> before the line </InitFile> into the ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno\ 1404/Engine.ini file. Rightclick on Anno 1404 in your Steam library -> properties -> launch options -> add: PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%.
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: Getting 100~280fps maxed out. FIX: in  a console paste this (you need to install winetricks first) : WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/33350/pfx/ winetricks d3dx9, then add "<DirectXVersion>9</DirectXVersion>" before the last </InitFile> into the ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Anno\ 1404/Engine.ini file. Rightclick on Anno 1404 in your Steam library -> properties -> launch options -> add: PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%. NB: Anti-aliasing will fuck up the ingame cinematics (D9VK only).
title: Anno 1404: Venice

notes: Installed dx9 like others have suggested. D9vk messed up those 3D characters.
